text: <CRITIC/videos> Engine issue, category-wide: the quiz/'safest' reveal crowns zero-count areas that are join artifacts (e.g. Atlanta's Bankhead — 0 incidents because the spatial join lands nothing there, not because it is safe). Proposed change: in the engine's quiz/safest derivation, exclude areas whose windowed total is 0 AND whose share of months with any activity is 0 (true no-data), or require a minimum incident floor; disclose the exclusion in the reveal caveat. Sweep: check ALL 20 cities for zero-count areas in the 60-month window before the next render wave. Why: prevents a factually-misleading 'safest' claim class in every future video.
producerNote: RESOLVED at the engine layer (root cause, not per-city patch). Root cause: derive.ts hoodRanking included polygons that received ZERO joined records of ANY category — no-data regions (name-join artifacts like APD's 'Historic Westin Heights/Bankhead' variant with no official polygon), which the tail-of-ranking 'safest' pick then crowned as safe. Fix: hoodRanking now excludes hoods with allTotal===0 (simpler + stricter than the proposed months-with-activity test — any-category zero over the whole window IS zero active months), and Reveal.tsx discloses the exclusion in the caveat only when it fires ('N areas with no mapped records excluded — no data isn’t “no crime”'). Quiz options + reveal both read the same ranking, so both are fixed by one change. SWEEP BREADTH: all 20 cities, mirroring the exact winBundle mapWindowMonths slice. Affected: atlanta-ga (Bankhead/Englewood Manor/Midwest Cascade 0 → answer now Bankhead Courts:3), milwaukee-wi (Brown Deer Park 0 → Zoo:1), washington-dc (Observatory Circle 0 → Rock Creek Park:15 — SHIPPED mp4 shows the artifact; re-render queued behind the citywide-trend audit HOLD, note filed in its feedback.json). 17 other cities unaffected (verified: no hood with allTotal===0; also zero hoods anywhere with 0 Group A but >0 'other', so the filter changes nothing else). Verified: tsc clean, validate.mjs PASS ×5, reveal stills atlanta/milwaukee (new answers + caveat) and detroit/denver (unchanged, no caveat), atlanta quiz still (options now Bankhead Courts/Brookwood Hills/Downtown). LESSON for detection: any ranking that rewards MINIMUM counts must distinguish 'zero because no data joined' from 'zero because nothing happened' — check every superlative claim ('fewest', 'safest', 'least') for the no-data→winner failure mode.
